name: Authentic Hue - Food, Culture & Countryside Discovery 3 days
highlights: Savor signature street food dishes on an evening cyclo foodie tour.
Glide down Perfume River by boat and take part in a traditional fish-releasing ritual.
Paddle a SUP at sunset on Southeast Asia’s largest lagoon and plant trees for sustainability.
Unwind with herbal foot baths and afternoon tea under grapefruit trees in Thuy Bieu Land.

itinerary.title: Hue Arrival -
itinerary.description: Upon arrival in Hue, you will be warmly greeted by your local guide and transferred to your hotel for check-in. Take some time to rest and refresh after your journey. Around 18:00 - 18:15, our tour guide and cyclo drivers will meet you at the designated meeting point in the city center to begin your evening food adventure. The first stop will be a Hue cake restaurant, where you will try the specialty cakes of the region, such as Banh Beo (bloating fern-shaped cakes). These soft, savory bites, topped with shrimp paste, crispy shallots, and fish sauce, are unforgettable favorites

itinerary.title: Hue Heritage Exploration by E-Scooter
itinerary.description: By 8:00, our car and guide will pick you up from the meeting point in the center of Hue. The day starts with a breath of fresh morning air as you come to An Hien Garden House, a treasured architectural work located right next to the poetic Perfume River. Soon after, you will move to Thien Mu Pagoda, the oldest and most spiritual site in Hue, standing for over 400 years. Here, you will spend time taking in the ancient and sacred architecture, the tranquil atmosphere, and the fascinating legends tied to this place. After that, you will stop at the Perfume River to take a boat trip

itinerary.title: Immersive Experience in Wet Rice Culture and Rural Life
itinerary.description: For the third day in Hue, you will experience a full day from 8:30 to 16:30 in Thanh Toan Village, with a chance to cycle up to 17 km on flat, peaceful roads. The cycling tour is designed to unveil the deep roots of Vietnamese rural life, dating back to the 17th century. By exploring this long-established village, you will gain insights into old Vietnamese community structures, local governance, and everyday customs that have been around for a long time. Throughout the day, you will have plenty of time to immerse yourself in countryside activities: farm in the rice fields, join locals in fishi...

included: Overnight in shared Twin/ Double room with Air-condition (AC) at hotel
All transport during tour on air - conditioned vehicle as per itinerary (including airport pick up & see off)
Meals as indicated in the program
